title: picea abies subsp. obovata
intro: Picea abies subsp. obovata, also known as Siberian spruce, is a subspecies of the common spruce tree (Picea abies) native to the mountainous regions of eastern Russia and northern China. This evergreen tree has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which are believed to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and combat respiratory infections. The essential oil extracted from Siberian spruce has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing stress, promoting relaxation, and exhibiting antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Siberian spruce is often used in herbal preparations, such as essential oils, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, skin conditions, and other health concerns.
